name: Stuffed Pork Chops
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup apple, peeled and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a deep pocket in each pork chop (see note).
2. Soak raisins in water for 5 minutes, then drain and finely chop.
3. Melt butter in a small skillet over low heat. Add onion and sauté about 3 minutes.
4. Add raisins and apple; cook for 1 minute.
5. In a large bowl, combine cornbread, salt, pepper, and sage. Stir in the apple mixture and beaten egg.
6. Stuff pork chops with the mixture. Fasten edges together with wooden picks.
7. Dip chops in remaining egg, then coat with flour, shaking off excess.
8. Heat oil in a large heavy skillet over medium-low heat.
9. Cook chops, turning occasionally, about 60 minutes or until juices run clear when pierced.
10. Remove wooden picks before serving.
Note: To make a pocket for stuffing in pork chops, cut a 2-inch slit in the fatty side of the chop with a sharp paring knife. Insert the knife into the slit and draw it back and forth toward the bone to create a large pocket along the length of the meat. Keep the opening as close to 2 inches as possible to prevent the stuffing from falling out while cooking. Secure the opening by inserting two wooden picks into the chop to form an X.
